calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Petition For Final Distribution Or Status Report.
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Distributing Funds Deposited With San Francisco Missing Heir Fund
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Mangini for and with (petitioner) Estate Research Associates (ERA) with Paul Elissiry (president) present.

The Court stated that the funds were escheated to the City Treasurer, that it understood that counsel would talk to the daughter regarding obtaining independent counsel, that counsel has filed the joinder, that the Court would be more comfortable if she had independent counsel and that it looks like ERA had a hand in preparing the document. Mr. Mangini stated that he was not of the understanding that she was to get independent counsel, that he helped with the document after getting her consent and that he had the document translated so that she could understand it. The Court stated that it is an inherent conflict of interest. Mr. Mangini reported that she understands how much money was found and how much ERA would earn. The Court asked if she was advised that she could contest the fees to ERA, that the other issues are if the fees are reasonable and whether it is appropriate to skip the wife's estate on the basis of the 13100 declarations filed. Mr. Elissiry stated that they have done this in Superior Court throughout the state of California. The Court stated that the payout needs to be requested by the heir and that it has never seen it done this way. Mr. Elissiry stated that he has routinely done this for 30 years and could provide case numbers for those done in this court. The Court stated that it is not happening here and that it has doubts that others have done it. Mr. Elissiry addressed the fee issue stating that they have done here what no one else has done, that both the Public Administrator and Japanese Consulate were unable to locate her and that the funds would have gone 100% to the government. The Court stated that it recognizes the value but it is not enough to justify the fee, that the fee needs to be justified by the hours spent, that ERA needs to keep track of its time, that if it does then it would then have something to hold up to the Court in determining whether to find if the fee is justified. Mr. Elissiry stated that they are now attempting to keep time records for the cases that they do in San Francisco, that his best estimate for this case is 120 hours plus expenses and that due to the value of the work 35% is not grossly unreasonable. The Court stated that this is a situation where there is not that much money in the end, that it is inclined to say that it is a reasonable fee and turned to the wife's share. Mr. Mangini stated that the daughter is the sole heir of her mother and that she filed a 13100 regarding the mother's share of the proceeds and that the fee that ERA collects includes all out of pocket expenses. The Court took the petition for order distributing funds deposited with San Francisco missing heir fund under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-28-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Weinkauf reported that there has been no word from Ms. Quintero or Ms. Mooney, that he has the names of the wife and child and will submit a revised order with the correct names. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-21-2014 Continued To Oct-20-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for petitioner.

Mr. Weinkauf stated that Ms. Quintero found other potential heirs, that she will be filing a petition to determine entitlement and that she is asking for a thirty day continuance. The accounting petition was continued to October 20, 2014 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-26-2014 Continued To Jun-02-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for Public Administrator.

The Court noted that Ms. Quintero was not present. Mr. Weinkauf requested a 90-day continuance so that he could subpoena records from the National Archives. The accounting petition was continued to June 2, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-15-2014 Continued To Feb-26-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Adriana Quintero for Donna Mooney (Objector). Steven Weinkauf for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court noted that Mr. Weinkauf's response to the Examiner's notes was received this morning and that another issue is whether there is any heir. Mr. Weinkauf stated they have found an old letter from Mr. Blewett's wife making reference of a daughter and that it is possible that the daughter lives in Japan. Discussions were held with regard to obtaining records from the National Archives. Ms. Quintero stated that her client wants to know if she has a cousin and that she requested for an order to the National Archives to release records. The Court instructed Ms. Ouintero to submit an Ex Parte Petition for an order to the National Archives to release records to Mr. Weinkauf. Mr. Weinkauf stated that when he received the records, he may have to file a Petition for Instructions to locate the heir. The matter was continued to February 26, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Order to Appear can go off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-15-2014 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-18-2013 Continued To Jan-15-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney to appear on January 15, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he has not responded to the Probate Examiner's notes and why notice has not been given to the Franchise Tax Board. The accounting petition is also continued to that date.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Clark. (Not Reported)
